title: 10 best herbal essential oils for jaw pain
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for jaw pain are plant-based extracts used to alleviate discomfort and inflammation in the jaw area. They work by reducing muscle tension and promoting relaxation, providing relief from pain and discomfort. The best herbal essential oils for jaw pain include Eucalyptus globulus, Mentha x piperita, Zingiber officinale, Gaultheria procumbens, Cymbopogon citratus, Lavandula angustifolia, Cinnamomum verum, Syzygium aromaticum, Melaleuca alternifolia, and Cinnamomum zeylanicum. If left untreated, jaw pain can disrupt daily life, causing difficulty sleeping, eating, and even speaking, leading to emotional distress and decreased productivity.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree essential oils helps with jaw pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and ease tension in the jaw muscles. When used topically, tea tree oil can be applied directly to the affected area, providing quick relief from discomfort and stiffness. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties can help to eliminate any underlying infections or inflammation that may be contributing to the pain.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial Properties: Tea Tree Essential Oil's antimicrobial properties can help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections in the gums, teeth, and jaw area, which may contribute to jaw pain.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and ease tension in the jaw muscles, ligaments, and joints, providing relief from chronic pain and discomfort.

Analgesic and Pain-Relieving Properties: Tea Tree Essential Oil has been shown to have analgesic and pain-relieving effects, which can help numb or reduce the sensation of pain in the jaw area, providing temporary relief from discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective for relieving jaw pain and tension.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that provide antioxidant and antibacterial benefits, helping to reduce inflammation and ease pain associated with jaw problems.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality melaleuca alternifolia leaves at dawn when they are most aromatic containing 20% of the total leaf material.
Dry the fresh leaves in a warm dark place for four to six hours or until they reach a moisture level below 10%.
Steam distill the dried leaves with a ratio of one part melaleuca alternifolia to two parts water at atmospheric pressure for three to four hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil and filter it through paper towels or cotton wool to remove impurities containing 5ml of extracted oil.
Store the pure melaleuca alternifolia essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids in a cool place for up to one year maintaining its quality.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass essential oils helps with jaw pain because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as citral and geraniol, work to reduce muscle tension and swelling in the jaw, providing relief from chronic conditions like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder. When inhaled or applied topically, lemongrass essential oil can also help to calm the mind and body, promoting relaxation and reducing stress that may be contributing to jaw pain.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Lemongrass essential oil's antispasmodic property helps to relax and calm muscle spasms, including those in the jaw muscles, providing relief from tension headaches and TMJ disorders.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of lemongrass essential oil can help reduce swelling and pain associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) dysfunction, a common cause of jaw pain.

Anxiolytic: Lemongrass essential oil's anxiolytic property has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate stress-induced tension headaches and jaw clenching, leading to relief from jaw pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate jaw pain and discomfort.
Rhyzomes: Containing citral and geranial, these compounds work together to reduce muscle tension and relax the jaw muscles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lemongrass leaves and chop them into small pieces weighing around 200 grams for optimal yield.
Steam distill the chopped lemongrass in a stainless steel still using 1000 milliliters of distilled water at atmospheric pressure.
Collect the resulting essential oil, filtering it through a paper filter or cotton cloth to remove impurities and debris.
Store the extracted essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature away from direct sunlight.
Perform quality control checks by verifying the oil's color clarity viscosity and aroma ensuring compliance with industry standards.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with jaw pain because it has natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that work to soothe and calm the muscles in the face, neck, and jaw. When applied topically or inhaled through steam inhalation, chamomile oil can help reduce tension and ease spasms, providing relief from discomfort and pain associated with conditions like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder, bruxism, and teeth grinding. Its calming effects also promote relaxation, further reducing overall stress and promoting a sense of well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile essential oil's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling and discomfort associated with jaw pain, such as TMJ disorder or temporomandibular joint syndrome.
Anxiolytic (anxiety-relieving) property: Chamomile essential oil's anxiolytic property can help calm the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety that may exacerbate jaw tension and pain.
Sedative property: Chamomile essential oil's sedative property helps promote relaxation and reduce muscle spasms in the jaw, which can contribute to chronic pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant are used to make essential oils for jaw pain because they contain a high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce swelling and ease tension in the jaw muscles.

Flowers: Chamomile flowers are used to make essential oils for jaw pain due to their soothing and calming properties, which can help to relax tense jaw muscles and alleviate symptoms of temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh flowers with optimal oil content and minimal moisture levels.
Steam distill the harvested flowers using a water-to-plant ratio of 1:4 for 30 minutes.
Collect and separate the obtained essential oil from the resulting hydrosol within 24 hours.
Filter the essential oil through a cotton cloth or coffee filter to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the final product in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, protected from light and heat.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Geranium essential oils helps with jaw pain because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, geranium oil can penetrate deep into the muscles and joints, providing relief from tension and discomfort caused by TMJ disorders. Its ability to relax and soothe the mind and body also helps reduce stress and anxiety, common contributors to jaw clenching and pain. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, geranium essential oils can effectively alleviate jaw pain and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Geranium oil's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and discomfort associated with jaw pain, such as temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.
Pain-relieving: The analgesic and numbing effects of geranium oil can help alleviate acute or chronic jaw pain by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body.
Antispasmodic: Geranium oil's ability to relax muscle spasms can help calm down tense facial muscles, reducing the frequency and severity of jaw pain and tension headaches.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate jaw pain caused by inflammation.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain rosmarinic acid, a compound that has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective for relieving jaw pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality pelargonium graveolens leaves and stems in the early morning after the dew has dried.
Wash the harvested plant material gently with cold water to remove dirt and debris then dry thoroughly.
Steam distill 100g of the dried plant material using 500ml of distilled water for 30-40 minutes to release essential oils.
Separate the essential oil from the distillation liquid through decantation or centrifugation within 1 hour.
Store the extracted pelargonium graveolens essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon essential oils helps with jaw pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. When inhaled, it has a profound impact on reducing swelling and discomfort associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders. The oil's analgesic qualities also help to numb the affected area, providing instant relief from throbbing jaw pain. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon essential oils have been known to relax tense muscles, further alleviating tension that can contribute to jaw pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Ceylon cinnamon essential oil contains cinnamaldehyde, which has potent anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort in the jaw area.
Analgesic property: The essential oil's analgesic properties can help numb the pain associated with jaw tension or temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder, providing relief from discomfort and pain.
Antispasmodic property: Ceylon cinnamon essential oil's antispasmodic effects can help relax muscle spasms in the jaw, face, and neck, which are common causes of jaw pain and tension.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the Ceylon cinnamon plant contain high concentrations of cinnamaldehyde, a compound known for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it an effective treatment for jaw pain.

Barks: The barks of the Ceylon cinnamon plant have been used for centuries to treat toothaches and other oral health issues, including jaw pain, due to their natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh cinnamomum verum bark in the morning when dew is still present on leaves.
Dry the harvested bark in a warm oven at 150°f for 3 hours or until crumbly texture achieved.
Blend 10 grams of dried bark with 100 milliliters of carrier oil like coconut or sweet almond oil.
Heat the blend gently at low temperature in a double boiler setup for 30 minutes to extract essential oils.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and discard solids before transferring the oil to dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange essential oils helps with jaw pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The oil's active compounds, such as limonene and beta-pinene, work to reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ), which is a common cause of jaw pain. Additionally, the oil's warming and relaxing effects help to calm muscle tension and spasms that can contribute to jaw pain, providing quick and effective relief for those suffering from chronic or acute pain in this area.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Bitter Orange Essential Oil contains anti-inflammatory compounds like limonene and citral, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with conditions like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders.
Analgesic: The oil's analgesic properties may help numb the pain in the jaw and surrounding areas, providing relief from discomfort and soreness.
Muscle relaxant: Bitter Orange Essential Oil can help relax tense muscles and reduce spasms in the jaw, neck, and face, which can contribute to conditions like bruxism or teeth grinding.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help alleviate jaw pain.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and limonoids that exhibit antiseptic and anti-inflammatory activities, making them effective in reducing inflammation and pain associated with jaw conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried citrus peel and 15g of dried bitter orange peel at dawn when the dew is still present on the plants.
Combine the harvested peels with 500ml of carrier oil in a clean glass jar, ensuring the peels are completely submerged in the liquid.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking it twice daily to facilitate the infusion process and allow for optimal extraction.
After the 14-day period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Transfer the resulting herbal essential oil to dark glass bottles and label them correctly, storing them in a cool dark place for up to 6 months before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with jaw pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and ease tension in the muscles. The oil's analgesic properties also work to alleviate discomfort and numbness, providing quick relief from jaw ache. Additionally, rosemary essential oil's ability to improve blood circulation may help to increase oxygen flow to the affected area, promoting healing and relaxation of the temporomandibular joint (TMJ). As a result, incorporating rosemary essential oils into your self-care routine can provide effective natural pain relief for jaw pain sufferers.
remedy_properties: Antinociceptive property: Rosemary essential oil's antinociceptive property helps to reduce pain by blocking the transmission of pain signals to the brain, providing relief from jaw pain.

Anti-inflammatory property: Rosemary essential oil's anti-inflammatory property reduces swelling and inflammation in the muscles and joints, including those involved in jaw pain, thereby alleviating discomfort and stiffness.

Analgesic property: Rosemary essential oil's analgesic property helps to reduce pain by stimulating the release of endorphins, the body's natural painkillers, providing temporary relief from jaw pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing jaw pain and inflammation.
Leaves: Rosemary leaves contain camphor and bornyl acetate, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate jaw pain and tension.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves at dawn or early morning when the aroma is most potent.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with filtered water and gentle soap to remove dirt and debris.
Steam-distill the washed leaves using a distillation apparatus for 4-6 hours to extract the essential oil.
Monitor the distillation process closely to ensure optimal temperature and pressure conditions are maintained.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities and sediment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with jaw pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. When inhaled, it stimulates the brain's limbic system, releasing mood-boosting neurotransmitters that help reduce tension and stress, common contributors to jaw pain. Additionally, the calming scent of lavender oil relaxes facial muscles, alleviating bruxism and TMJ disorders that can lead to jaw pain. As a result, English lavender essential oils offer natural relief for individuals suffering from jaw discomfort, promoting relaxation and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: English Lavender essential oil's antispasmodic property helps to relax muscles and reduce spasms, which can alleviate jaw tension and pain caused by conditions like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.
Analgesic: The analgesic properties of English Lavender essential oil help to numb the pain associated with jaw issues, providing relief from discomfort and inflammation in the jaw area.

Anti-inflammatory: English Lavender essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the jaw joint and surrounding muscles, making it a natural remedy for conditions like arthritis or gum disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a popular choice for making essential oils, as they contain a higher concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate jaw pain.
Flowers: Lavender flowers are often used in aromatherapy blends to calm the mind and body, reducing tension and discomfort associated with jaw pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavender flowers at their peak fragrance and color.
Dry the harvested lavender flowers using a food dehydrator for 12 hours or air drying for 7-10 days.
Crush the dried lavender flowers into small pieces using a mortar and pestle to release their essential oils.
Steam distill the crushed lavender flowers in a still using 30% water and 70% steam at 100°c for 2-3 hours.
Filter and decant the resulting essential oil from the condenser, storing it in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with jaw pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The oil's active compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, work to reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the jaw muscles and joints. Additionally, thyme oil's warming and relaxing effects can help calm tense jaw muscles, releasing tension and relieving pain associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders and other conditions. By applying topically or inhaling, thyme essential oil provides fast-acting relief from jaw pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial Properties: Thyme oil's antibacterial properties help combat infections caused by bacteria in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to jaw pain and inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: Thyme oil's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders, helping to relieve jaw tension and discomfort.
Analgesic Properties: Thyme oil's analgesic properties provide temporary relief from pain, making it effective in reducing the discomfort of jaw pain caused by TMJ disorders or other dental issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain a high concentration of antioxidants and volatile compounds, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate jaw pain.
Buds: Thyme buds are rich in essential oil-producing glands, making them an ideal choice for creating medicinal essential oils that target jaw pain and tension.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and stems in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the plant material by spreading it thinly on paper towels for 7-10 days, stirring occasionally.
Steam distill 1000 ml of water with the dried plant material for 60 minutes to extract essential oils.
Collect the resulting essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities.
Transfer the filtered essential oil to dark glass bottles and store them in a cool, dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with jaw pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. When inhaled, fennel oil reduces inflammation and relaxes tense muscles in the face and neck, easing tension headaches and temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders that can cause jaw pain. Additionally, fennel's anesthetic properties help numb the pain receptors, providing quick relief from acute discomfort. By applying topically or inhaling its aroma, fennel essential oil provides a safe and natural remedy for alleviating jaw pain and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Fennel essential oil has been shown to reduce inflammation, which can alleviate swelling and pain associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders, a common cause of jaw pain.

Anesthetic and analgesic properties: The essential oil contains compounds that have a numbing effect on the skin and mucous membranes, helping to reduce sensitivity and ease discomfort in the jaw area.

Muscle relaxant property: Fennel essential oil has been found to relax muscles and improve circulation, which can help reduce tension and spasms in the jaw, neck, and face, all of which are common contributors to jaw pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are rich in compounds that help alleviate muscle tension and spasms, making them effective for relieving jaw pain.
Buds: Fennel buds contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh fennel leaves and stems on a sunny morning for optimal oil content.
Cleanly chop the harvested fennel material into small pieces to release its natural oils and aromas.
Steam distill the chopped fennel in a copper still at atmospheric pressure for 30 minutes with 100 milliliters of distilled water.
Collect and separate the resulting essential oil from the distillation process using a condenser and a separating funnel, yielding 5 milliliters of high-quality foeniculum vulgare oil.
Store the prepared foeniculum vulgare essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for up to six months, protected from direct sunlight and heat sources.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat jaw pain with herbal essential oils is by applying a blend of oils topically to the affected area. Lavender oil's calming properties help reduce tension, while peppermint oil's cooling effect alleviates discomfort. Chamomile oil soothes inflammation, while eucalyptus oil's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling. Mix equal parts of these oils with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and gently massage onto the jawline for effective relief from pain and stiffness.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jaw pain that are treated with herbal essential oils are headaches, migraines, and facial tension. Peppermint oil can help alleviate tension and soothe headache symptoms, while lavender oil promotes relaxation and reduces stress. Eucalyptus oil is often used to treat sinus pressure and congestion, which can contribute to headaches and facial pain.
